title: Auburn
name: Auburn
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Auburn's Boeing fabrication plant, Green River Valley distribution stock, and Outlet Collection retail.

sections.html: Auburn extends the Kent Valley's industrial base further south along the Green River, and a buyer building an Auburn identification package should expect distribution and manufacturing product to make up most of the submittal, with the Outlet Collection Seattle adding a smaller retail component. A qualified intermediary coordinating the closing should confirm scale honestly against Kent's larger inventory before the 45-day identification window closes, since Auburn trades at a real but smaller scale than its neighbor to the north and should be evaluated on its own terms rather than as a discount version of Kent.

sections.heading: Boeing's Auburn Fabrication Plant and Its Supplier Ring
sections.html: Boeing's Auburn plant fabricates structural components rather than assembling complete aircraft, and the supplier and machining tenants around it lease space suited to heavy manufacturing rather than general distribution. A buyer identifying industrial space near this plant should confirm power service and floor-load capacity separately from a typical Kent Valley distribution building, since fabrication tenants place different demands on a building than a pallet-storage tenant does. A buyer should also ask whether current equipment sits on a lease from the tenant or is bolted into the building itself, since that distinction affects what remains if the fabrication tenant does not renew, and an appraiser should be told explicitly which category applies before finalizing a valuation.

sections.heading: Green River Valley Distribution Stock
sections.html: Auburn's stretch of the Green River Valley carries newer distribution buildings along the SR-167 corridor that lease to regional logistics and e-commerce fulfillment tenants overflowing from Kent's more built-out core. This stock trades at a discount to comparable Kent Valley product, largely reflecting a slightly longer drive time to the port and to Seattle, and a buyer should reflect that discount in underwriting rather than pricing an Auburn building as if it sat inside Kent proper. Newer buildings along this stretch tend to match Kent's clear-height and ESFR sprinkler standards even where land values run lower, so a buyer should not assume older specifications simply because Auburn trades at a discount.

sections.heading: Outlet Collection Seattle and Surrounding Retail
sections.html: The Outlet Collection Seattle anchors Auburn's retail base and draws shoppers from across the south Puget Sound region rather than from Auburn alone, giving this property a regional rather than neighborhood retail profile. A buyer identifying retail here should pull comparables from other regional outlet centers rather than from Auburn's smaller neighborhood strip centers, since the two trade on entirely different fundamentals. Smaller retail pads surrounding the outlet property do benefit from its traffic, and a buyer should confirm a specific pad's lease terms and co-tenancy provisions before assuming it shares the anchor property's demand profile outright.

sections.heading: Building the Auburn Identification List
sections.html:
A realistic Auburn submittal typically draws from:

  • heavy-manufacturing and fabrication-support industrial near the Boeing plant
  • distribution buildings along the SR-167 Green River Valley corridor
  • regional retail centered on the Outlet Collection
  • smaller neighborhood retail and flex space

sections.heading: Pricing Auburn Honestly Against Kent Valley
sections.html:
A buyer should resist pricing an Auburn industrial building off Kent Valley comparables without adjustment, since the modest drive-time difference and Auburn's smaller overall inventory both show up in cap rates, and an appraiser working from Kent-only comparables can overstate an Auburn building's value.

Buyers often pair an Auburn identification with a Kent Valley alternative on the same three-property list, keeping both Green River Valley submarkets represented so a financing delay on one does not leave the exchange without a workable option inside the 180-day exchange period. If more than three candidates make the list across both submarkets, the qualified intermediary should confirm the 200% rule against combined value, or track the 95% rule if the buyer intends to acquire nearly all of what was identified.

faqs.q: How does Boeing's Auburn plant differ from its Renton or Everett plants?
faqs.a: Auburn fabricates structural components rather than assembling complete aircraft, so the surrounding supplier tenants need heavy-manufacturing specifications like power service and floor-load capacity rather than general distribution features, which is a different diligence checklist than a buyer would use for a Renton or Everett candidate.

faqs.q: Does Auburn industrial trade at the same price as Kent Valley industrial?
faqs.a: No, Auburn typically trades at a discount reflecting its slightly longer drive time to the port and Seattle, and a buyer should adjust comparables accordingly rather than pricing off Kent-only data, even though newer Auburn buildings often match Kent's clear-height and sprinkler specifications.

faqs.q: What kind of retail does the Outlet Collection Seattle represent?
faqs.a: A regional draw pulling shoppers from across the south Puget Sound area rather than a neighborhood center, so comparables should come from other regional outlet centers, not Auburn's smaller strip retail, though nearby pads that benefit from its traffic deserve a closer look at their own lease terms.

faqs.q: Is Auburn's industrial inventory as large as Kent's?
faqs.a: No, Auburn is smaller than Kent, and a buyer should size expectations and comparable pools accordingly rather than assuming Kent-scale inventory or pricing, though newer Auburn buildings frequently match Kent's clear-height and sprinkler specifications despite the smaller overall footprint.

faqs.q: Why pair an Auburn identification with Kent Valley?
faqs.a: Both sit along the same Green River Valley industrial corridor, so pairing them keeps a workable option available if financing or diligence on one candidate runs past a comfortable timeline, and the pricing gap between the two submarkets gives a buyer some flexibility on basis matching.
